packets.title: Morning dispatch and blocked-work recovery
packets.purpose: Specify a buildable supervisory dispatch surface that prioritizes current work and recovers partially completed vineyard work without erasing its operational or audit history.
preconditions: The work order resolves to one stable block identity
Every state change has an actor and timestamp
Target and actual acreage use the same unit

state_contract.history_policy: Append transitions; never rewrite blocked, partial, or correction events after later completion.

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is blocked and reported acreage is valid
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append partial completion with actor, time, acreage, and reason

interaction_contract.precondition: Repair evidence exists and current state is partially_completed
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append release event without changing the blocker record

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is completed and actual acres equal target acres
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append supervisor verification with actor and time

interaction_contract.precondition: User can view the work order

error_messages: Repair evidence is required before releasing the remaining rows.
Completed acreage cannot exceed the block acreage.
The work order changed elsewhere; review the newer status before retrying.

acceptance_criteria: The screen exposes blocked reason, affected rows, reporter, and time without opening a secondary record.
Partial acreage survives resume and final completion as an immutable historical event.
Only a vineyard manager can transition completed work to verified.
The rendered slice resolves WF-001, SCN-001-01, FIX-001, SCR-001, CMP-001, and PKT-001.
Status meaning remains understandable without color and at a 375 CSS-pixel viewport.

test_cases.assertion: Assigned work advances to in progress with an attributable event.
A blocker preserves 11.2 completed acres and rows 28–34 as open.
Repair evidence releases only the remaining rows and retains prior history.
A foreman cannot perform the manager-only verification transition.
Every operational state has a visible text label and non-color cue.
Final actual acreage equals the 18.6-acre target and never exceeds block acreage.
